render_evidence.basis: golden-parity gate run 2026-07-06 (source-anchor replay): structural 0.97297 (5/5 critical), pixel 1.0 (4 ROIs: whole-chart ink-colors + hue, left/right staircase clusters; same-geometry back-to-back captures), composite 98, binding-sanity sane via reconciled Copy>Data mark counts 17=17 (Superstore Sub-Category) on source and applied renders; running total verified live (status bar Running Sum of SUM(Profit) 4,117,943) | style-delta re-verify 2026-07-07: CookBook dark theme removed (black table background; choropleth also re-enabled base map layers). STRUCTURE unchanged. Live re-verified: bind(no-LLM)->inject->apply->render on Sample - Superstore, marks correct, light theme + map layers visually confirmed (W60). Pixel parity vs the dark source golden intentionally diverges on background/base-layer attrs.
render_evidence.lane: fable-direct overnight stamp factory 2026-07-06 (orchestrator live gate run)
render_evidence.pixel_oracle: source render (same-geometry back-to-back captures on the live instance; source and applied are two INDEPENDENT renders of the same data)
render_evidence.run: hidden source sheet surfaced via unhide+activate .twb bypass; bind gate honestly escalated not-fast-path pre-stamp; apply via direct placeholder fill (native zero-substitution bindings); one transient 'Operation cancelled' transport failure re-issued per classifier guidance

description: A waterfall (bridge) chart: GanttBar floating bars whose tops sit on a running total ([cum:sum:Profit:qk] on rows) and whose lengths are the negative per-category measure (-SUM([Profit]) on size), so each category shows its incremental contribution to a cumulative whole across an ordered categorical axis (Sub-Category on cols, sorted DESC by SUM(Profit)). Color is a continuous SUM(Profit) encoding. Factory-compiled from the CookBook 'Waterfall Viz' worksheet; structurally compiled and render-verified (live-2026-07-06).
avoid_when: The ask is about a smooth trend or trajectory over continuous time — a line/area chart shows the running trajectory better than a waterfall's discrete running-total bars.

The bound categorical has no meaningful order and you do not sort it by the measure — the running total is order-dependent, so an arbitrary member order yields an arbitrary cumulative path (this template sorts Sub-Category DESC by SUM(Profit); binding an unordered dimension without that sort breaks the bridge).
You only want to compare per-category magnitudes (not their cumulative build-up) — a ranked bar chart compares values more directly than a running-total waterfall.

slots.notes: The measure whose per-category contributions build the waterfall. Bound as SUM on color ([sum:Profit:qk]); the SAME base [Profit] also feeds the running-total [cum:sum:Profit:qk] on rows (a CumTotal table calc that rides along — hazard running-total-table-calc-rides-along) and the -SUM([Profit]) bar-length calc (calcs[0]). replaceFieldReferences rewrites every [Profit] instance on bind.

Categorical axis on cols — one floating bar per member, ordered by the computed-sort (DESC by SUM(Profit)); the running total accumulates left-to-right across these members. XML column-instance [none:Sub-Category:nk].

calcs.notes: Template-owned GanttBar length: -SUM([Profit]) sizes each floating bar downward from its running-total row anchor so the bar spans this category's delta. A self-closing <calculation>, so parseTemplateCalcs sees it — a declared calc slot. Its one input is the bindable 'profit' slot; replaceFieldReferences rewrites [Profit] on bind.

hazards.detail: The rows axis [cum:sum:Profit:qk] is a <column-instance> of base [Profit] carrying a nested <table-calc type='CumTotal'> (Running Total). Because it is a column-INSTANCE with a nested table-calc (not a <column><calculation>), parseTemplateCalcs (self-closing <calculation> only) never sees it — it is NOT a declared calc slot and rides along verbatim on rows. Its base is the bindable 'profit' slot; replaceFieldReferences rewrites [Profit] inside the instance on bind. The running total is ORDER-DEPENDENT (accumulates across Sub-Category in the computed-sort order), so an unordered bound dimension yields an arbitrary cumulative path. Same class as ranking-bump-chart table-calc-rank-rides-along and slope-chart slope-filter-table-calc.

The source workbook carries a datasource-level <style-rule element='mark'> value->hue/glyph map (3 color / 4 shape maps, factory-detected) referencing Sub-Category and other workbook-shared fields. It is NOT carried into this template (no datasource_style sidecar) because it is irrelevant to this waterfall: the color that matters is the continuous SUM(Profit) encoding fully contained in the worksheet (<color column='[sum:Profit:qk]'>), NOT a categorical Sub-Category hue. Carrying it would also splice customer member values + federated datasource IDs into the repo (licensing/sensitive-data wall) — the same decision L1 made for spatial-choropleth-map. No fidelity loss for this template; on apply only the in-worksheet encoding is applied.

Product evidence (P&L waterfalls): real revenue/margin waterfalls carry MID-CHAIN SUBTOTAL members — e.g. 'Gross Profit' after the revenue/COGS block, then opex steps, then 'Operating Income'. This template treats EVERY member of the bound dimension as an independent step, adding its -SUM([Profit]) delta to the running total. A subtotal row is not an increment — it restates the running total to date — so if it is present as a member it DOUBLE-COUNTS: the cumulative path jumps by the subtotal's full value and every step after it is offset. The template cannot distinguish a subtotal member from a step member. Detection/guidance: bind a dimension of INCREMENTAL line items only (exclude subtotal/total members via a filter before binding), or model subtotals as reference lines / separate annotation rather than as steps. A tell-tale is a running total that ends at roughly 2x the true total, or a member whose delta equals the sum of the preceding steps.

Product evidence (product team, 2026-07) — has bitten someone: the running total only MEANS something when the bound category carries an EXPLICIT or SEMANTIC order (a P&L line-item sequence, a time order, or the computed sort this template applies: Sub-Category DESC by SUM(Profit)). Bind a nominal dimension with NO inherent order and let it fall back to ALPHABETICAL member order (e.g. product names A→Z) and the cumulative path becomes an arbitrary artifact of the labels — rename or re-sort the same rows and the bridge tells a different story. Bind an ordered/ordinal dimension or keep the measure sort; never read a cumulative build-up off an unordered alphabetical nominal. Tracked-hazard companion to the order-dependence avoid_when.

Product evidence (product team, 2026-07) — has bitten someone: when the source rows carry a MIXED currency_code (USD + EUR + GBP …), the bound measure must NOT be summed raw. The running total [cum:sum:Profit:qk] on rows and every -SUM([Profit]) bar delta add different currency units as one number, so the cumulative whole is silently WRONG (a data-correctness bug, not a formatting one). The template has no currency awareness. Convert to a single reporting currency, or filter to one currency_code, BEFORE binding a revenue/margin measure into the waterfall.
